title: Phase-shift instanton approach to tunneling duality in Read--Rezayi state
authors: Ryoi Ohashi, Hiroki Isobe, Ryota Nakai, Kentaro Nomura
abstract: We study the duality between quasi-particle and electron tunneling in point-contact geometries of fractional quantum Hall states. To treat non-Abelian edge operators, we introduce a "phase-shift instanton" that incorporates phase factors from primary fields into the instanton gas framework. Using this method, we reformulate the Moore--Read duality and obtain an explicit dual description for the $k=3$ Read-Rezayi state. Our results clarify how quasi-particle tunneling produces characteristic phase shifts in instantons and how these shifts map strong quasi-particle tunneling to weak electron tunneling. Based on this dual description, we analytically evaluate the non-linear differential conductance in the strong-coupling regime. We reveal that, due to the physical requirement that the tunneling particle across the vacuum gap must be a true fermion, the transport behavior universally converges to a $G \propto V^4$ scaling for both the Moore--Read and Read--Rezayi states. This universal transport signature highlights a fundamental topological constraint underlying non-Abelian fractional quantum Hall edges.
summary: This paper develops a phase-shift instanton method to establish a duality between quasi-particle and electron tunneling in non-Abelian Read-Rezayi fractional quantum Hall states. It discovers that the strong-coupling regime exhibits a universal V^4 conductance scaling across different states due to fundamental topological constraints. This result implies that simple conductance measurements cannot distinguish between these complex non-Abelian states in the strong-coupling limit.
main_problem: Extending the concept of tunneling duality from Abelian and Moore-Read states to the more complex non-Abelian Read-Rezayi states, specifically addressing the difficulty of the intricate operator algebra.
main_result: The authors derive an explicit dual description for the k=3 Read-Rezayi state and reveal a universal transport signature where differential conductance scales as G proportional to V^4 in the strong-coupling regime.
method: A new 'phase-shift instanton' approach is introduced to incorporate phase factors from non-Abelian primary fields into an instanton gas framework, combined with Renormalization Group (RG) analysis.
model_or_system: The system consists of fractional quantum Hall (FQH) states (Laughlin, Moore-Read, and Read-Rezayi) in point-contact geometries, modeled using effective edge actions involving U(1) charge sectors and Z_k parafermion Conformal Field Theory (CFT).
key_observables: Non-linear differential conductance (G) and tunneling current (I) as a function of bias voltage (V).
important_parameters: Filling factors (nu = 1/3, 1/2, 3/5), tunneling amplitudes (QPT and ET), and scaling dimensions (Delta).
main_assumptions: The method assumes the low-frequency limit where the frequency dependence of the Fourier coefficient can be neglected, and it relies on the instanton-gas approximation.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows point-contact geometries and the concept of tunneling duality; Figure 3 illustrates the phase-shift instanton process for Moore-Read and Read-Rezayi states.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of tunneling duality, presents the phase-shift instanton method, applies it to Moore-Read and Read-Rezayi states, performs RG analysis for conductance scaling, and provides mathematical derivations in the appendices.
why_interesting: The paper provides deep insights into the topological constraints of non-Abelian edge states and demonstrates how complex anyonic operators can recombine into a universal fermion, which is relevant to the study of many-body dynamics and topological order.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Illustration of the point-contact geometry in FQH systems. (a) and (b) show QPT and ET processes at a point contact, respectively. (c) highlights the tunneling duality.
FIG. 2. Schematic picture of the instanton and anti- instanton solutions in the periodic potential of Eq. (22). Solid (dotted) arrow represents the (anti-)instanton solution.

FIG. 3. Schematic picture of the phase-shift instanton pro- cess. We define ”phase-shift instanton” as the instanton pro- cess that occurs with a phase-shift of the potential. Upper figure shows the potential of Eqs. (31) and (38). Lower figure represents the details of the phase-shift instanton process in each case. Solid (dotted) line represents the potential after (before) the instanton process. (a) shows the Moore–Read state case with π-shift instanton, and (b) shows the Read– Rezayi state case with 2π/3-shift instanton.
